title: If you disagree with an ATO decision
description: You can dispute or object to our decision if you disagree with it.
keywords: amendment, decision, dispute, external review, objection, penalties, remit, remission, review, review process,

child_links.title: Which dispute or objection option is right for you
child_links.description: Find the right option for your situation.

child_links.title: Dispute a decision
child_links.description: Explains how we work collaboratively with taxpayers to resolve issues quickly and efficiently.

child_links.title: Object to a decision
child_links.description: When and how to object to decisions we make about your tax affairs, and which decisions you can object to.

child_links.title: Dispute interest or penalties
child_links.description: You can ask us to remit (reduce) interest charges or remit (reduce) or cancel penalties on unpaid tax liabilities.

child_links.title: Seek an external review of ATO decisions
child_links.description: You may have ATO decisions reviewed or appealed by the Tax Ombudsman, Administrative Review Tribunal or Federal Court.

child_links.title: Settlement
child_links.description: When and how settlement may be used to avoid or resolve disputes.

source_title: If you disagree with an ATO decision | Australian Taxation Office
source_description: If you disagree with an ATO decision. You can dispute or object to our decision if you disagree with it. Which dispute or objection option is right for you.
